title: Can American customers expect any benefit as US companies seek tariff refunds?
summary: The US government has launched the CAPE portal for businesses to apply for tariff refunds after SCOTUS deemed Trump's tariff regime illegal. However, companies are unlikely to pass on the benefit to Americans.

title: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme vs Fixed Deposits: Which gives better interest rates over five-year investment period?
summary: Among the safest investment tools in India, bank deposits and the government's Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) are the preferred choice for retired investors seeking steady payouts and guaranteed returns.

title: Direct funds vs regular funds: Differences, key things to remember, and which option investors should choose
summary: Direct funds are plans where investors apply directly with a fund house without an agent, bank or broker. While this eliminates commission fees, they demand more markets knowledge from investors. Here's what you need to keep in mind…

title: Mutual fund investment: From expense to risk — These are the factors you must consider before choosing a fund
summary: Mutual fund investments are considered among the best ways for an ordinary investor to book capital gains. However, there are varying levels of risk attached based on the type of scheme. Here's what you should consider before picking one.

title: DA hike: Finance Ministry increases Dearness Allowance for central govt employees by 2% wef 1 January 2026
summary: The Ministry of Finance on 22 April announced revision of rates of Dearness Allowance (DA) for central government employees, effective from 1 January 2026. Here's all we know so far…

title: Savings of ₹50 lakh? How inflation and lifestyle determine if your money lasts or runs out
summary: A big reality check on ₹50 lakh in savings in India shows how inflation, lifestyle expenses, and location determine how long the corpus lasts and whether it builds long-term financial security.&nbsp;

title: Income-tax returns: Here's how to declare gifts, mutual fund and share transfers in ITR form
summary: Recipients of significant financial gifts from relatives should disclose these in their ITR to ensure transparency. Proper reporting and retention of documents can prevent tax queries or notices from the tax department in the future.

title: Indian retail portfolios are no longer insulated from global shocks
summary: As the correlation between Indian and US markets hits record highs, the hidden global risks embedded in domestic stocks are making traditional diversification strategies obsolete.

title: In a fragile world, how anti-fragile is your portfolio?
summary: The world is unpredictable—build a portfolio that benefits from shocks.&nbsp;

title: NRI rental income compliance: Why is my tenant asking for UK address and tax ID from April 2026?
summary: New Income-tax Act, 2025 and Rules, 2026 change foreign remittance reporting. What NRIs renting out Indian property must disclose—and whether PPF withdrawals remain tax-free.

title: US stocks today: US market rises on Iran ceasefire extension and solid earnings
summary: U.S. stocks rallied Wednesday, snapping a two-day decline, as President Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran. Despite ongoing uncertainty and Iran's seizure of ships, positive earnings reports and strong tech sector performance boosted investor sentiment. The market remains hopeful for a beneficial

title: US stocks: FICO shares slide as Freddie Mac, Fannie Mae begin accepting rival VantageScore
summary: FICO shares dropped 13% after Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae announced they will accept mortgages scored by rival VantageScore 4.0. This move aims to reduce homebuyer costs and increase competition in the credit scoring market. The housing giants will begin with a limited rollout to approved lenders.

title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
summary: Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex declined, snapping a three-day rally due to IT and FMCG losses. Rising India VIX signaled caution, though broader trends remain positive with key support and resistance levels in focus.

title: Market Trading Guide: Buy Adani Green and HBL Engineering on Thursday for gains up to 16%
summary: Nifty 50 ended lower, breaking its winning streak amid IT and FMCG weakness. Technical indicators show mixed signals with bearish bias short term. Analysts highlight support levels and recommend selective buying in Adani Green Energy and HBL Engineering.

title: Only a handful of traders power India’s F&O volumes, highlights Zerodha's Nithin Kamath
summary: Nithin Kamath said India’s F&O market remains small despite perceptions, with limited participation. A tiny group of 1–2% traders drives 60–70% of volumes, making brokerage revenues highly dependent on a narrow, concentrated investor base.

title: India denies cash, crypto payments to Iran for Hormuz passage
summary: India has denied making any payments, in cash or cryptocurrency, to Iran for safe passage of its vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. This clarification follows an incident on April 18 where two Indian vessels were fired upon by Iranian forces and forced to turn back.

title: BitDelta India launches institutional-grade VDA infrastructure, targets trust-led growth in crypto market
summary: BitDelta India launched as an FIU-registered VDASP, targeting India’s growing crypto market with institutional-grade infrastructure, strong security, and compliance. The platform offers unified INR trading, advanced technology, and aims to drive trust-led participation beyond metro cities.

title: US Stocks: Traders place $430 million bet on lower oil price before Trump ceasefire extension
summary: Traders placed a series of bets worth $430 million on a drop ​in crude prices just ​15 minutes before U.S. President Donald Trump said he would extend a ​ceasefire with Iran on Tuesday.

title: US Stocks: Boeing posts smaller-than-expected quarterly loss as recovery gains traction
summary: Boeing on Wednesday reported a ​much smaller first-quarter loss than analysts expected, a sign of continued operational recovery at the U.S. planemaker after years of crises that dented its reputation and left it with a mountain of debt.

title: US Stocks: Blockchain billionaire Sun takes Trump family’s crypto firm to court
summary: Crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun on Tuesday sued World Liberty Financial, the digital currency venture co-founded by U.S. President Donald Trump and ​his sons, alleging that World Liberty illegally froze his holdings of tokens issued by the company.
